channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Key Changes in Worker Rights Under the Employment Rights Act 2025
item.description: The Employment Rights Act 2025 is being gradually implemented throughout 2026 and 2027, introducing several significant protections for workers. Starting now, employees gain expanded statutory sick pa...

item.title: Results of the US Powerball Draw on 6 April
item.description: The US Powerball draw held on Monday, 6 April, revealed the winning numbers as 7, 24, 37, 42, and 57, with the Powerball number being 5. The Power Play multiplier for this draw was 2. The jackpot was ...

item.title: Daily Horoscopes and Guidance for April 6, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail’s April 6, 2026, daily horoscope by Sally Brompton offers individual forecasts for each zodiac sign, encouraging focus, balance, and emotional awareness. The article begins with a m...

item.title: The challenges and exploitation faced by international students in UK universities
item.description: British universities have become increasingly dependent on international students, who pay significantly higher fees than domestic counterparts, generating a quarter of total university income. This f...

item.title: Scientist Advocates for a More Curious, Less Production-Driven Approach to Research
item.description: Physicist and science communicator Miguel Peiró argues against the growing trend of scientific research solely focused on economic gain and productivity. In his upcoming book, *Sombra y asombro: manif...

item.title: Shootout outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul leaves one attacker dead and two injured
item.description: On Tuesday, a serious shootout occurred outside Israel’s consulate in Istanbul, resulting in the death of one gunman and injuries to two others, as well as minor injuries to two police officers, accor...

item.title: Vietnam’s Communist Party leader To Lam elected president, consolidating power in echo of China and Laos
item.description: Vietnam has unanimously elected To Lam, the General Secretary of the Communist Party, as the country’s president for a five-year term. This decision marks a significant departure from Vietnam’s long-s...

item.title: Elderly Man Shares Heartbreaking Story of Losing His Property Over a Relationship
item.description: An elderly man from Tanzania became an internet sensation after a heartfelt performance of a song by Tanzanian artist Diameter. His rendition of 'Fala' resonated deeply with many, as it reflected his ...

item.title: Rajya Sabha Chairman rejects opposition's impeachment motion against Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar
item.description: In a significant development in Indian politics, Rajya Sabha Chairman CP Radhakrishnan rejected the impeachment motion brought by the opposition against Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar on Mo...

item.title: U.S. Military Declares Space Superiority Over Iran Amid Limited Iranian Capabilities
item.description: The U.S. military recently declared it has achieved 'space superiority' over Iran, a statement that has drawn scrutiny from defense analysts. This declaration followed Operation Epic Fury, which repor...

item.title: Picsart Introduces Program to Help Creators Earn Revenue from AI Content
item.description: Picsart, an AI-powered design platform, has introduced a creator monetization program aimed at rewarding creative efforts rather than follower count. The program is open to all creators, without any m...

item.title: Benny Maverick honours his mother with new album Valencia
item.description: Durban-born DJ and entrepreneur Benny Maverick has released his latest album, Valencia, which he describes as a tribute to his mother and the source of his resilience. In an interview with Sowetan, Be...

item.title: Long COVID impacts nearly 200,000 New Zealanders as experts call for urgent government response
item.description: The latest New Zealand Health Survey reveals that almost 200,000 adults are living with long COVID, highlighting the ongoing effects of the pandemic six years after it began. Around 77.7 percent of Ki...

item.title: Overview of Northlands Aerodrome within Kenyatta Family's Northlands City Project
item.description: Northlands Aerodrome is a private airstrip located within the expansive Northlands City development in Ruiru, about 15 km from Nairobi. The airstrip is part of the Kenyatta family's KSh500 billion mix...

item.title: Dodoma patients receive support from Finance Ministries' annual outreach
item.description: In Dodoma, a total of 38 women admitted in the maternity ward and 44 children under five in the paediatric ward received humanitarian assistance from the Ministry of Finance in partnership with Zanzib...

item.title: Children Encounter Coded Predator Language Online Before Parents Recognize It
item.description: The article describes how children are increasingly encountering coded terms related to predatory behavior online, often before their parents are even aware of them. One mother discovered this when he...

item.title: Court of Appeal Defers Hearing in Consumer Suit Against P&G and Kimberly-Clark Over Product Labelling
item.description: The Court of Appeal in Kenya has postponed proceedings in a major consumer rights case involving more than 2,800 Kenyans against global companies Procter & Gamble and Kimberly-Clark. The case was file...

item.title: Drama Festival Bans Political Content, School Disqualified Over Singapore Reference
item.description: The 2026 Kenya National Drama and Film Festival has introduced strict new guidelines banning political messaging, following controversies over plays with political content in the past. The revised rul...

item.title: Artemis II crew viewed Ryan Gosling’s Project Hail Mary prior to historic lunar mission
item.description: Before embarking on their landmark lunar voyage, the Artemis II astronauts found a moment of relaxation by watching Ryan Gosling’s new science fiction film, *Project Hail Mary*. Jeremy Hansen, one of ...

item.title: Trump’s Strategy Toward Iran Reflects Nixon’s ‘Madman Theory’ Approach
item.description: The article examines how former U.S. President Donald Trump’s handling of the conflict with Iran may mirror Richard Nixon’s 'Madman Theory'—a Cold War tactic designed to keep adversaries uncertain by ...
